title: SA PDPL Art. 6 — processing-purpose and lawful-basis evidence
exclusions: Does NOT substitute for the full regulatory control set; this UC provides one of several evidence streams and must be combined with policy, procedure, and supplemental UCs to demonstrate the complete clause.
evidence: Scheduled search output persisted to the compliance summary index with regulation=SA PDPL, clause=Art. 6, available as a saved search and exportable as a CSV evidence pack for the responsible officer.

compliance.assurance_rationale: SA PDPL Art. 6 — UC provides continuous Splunk telemetry evidence for this regulatory requirement, uplifted from contributing to partial after review confirmed the SPL captures relevant monitoring signals.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SA PDPL Art. 6 (Lawful grounds and consent for processing) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.50.12: SA PDPL Art. 6 — processing-purpose and lawful-basis evidence.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.50.12' running on Consent management platform logs, downstream processing system logs tagged with purpose., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.
compliance.smeCaveat: Phase-C draft: SME review required before uplifting assurance from 'contributing' to 'partial' or 'full'. Confirm that the SPL captures the specific regulator expectation and that the telemetry exists in the deployment.

dataSources: Consent management platform logs, downstream processing system logs tagged with purpose.
spl:
index=consent sourcetype=cmp:events earliest=-24h
| eval lawful_basis=coalesce(lawful_basis,"unknown")
| stats count AS consented BY subject_id, purpose_id, lawful_basis
| join type=left purpose_id
    [ search index=app_processing earliest=-24h sourcetype=processing:events | stats count AS processed BY subject_id, purpose_id ]
| eval unlawful=if(isnotnull(processed) AND consented=0, "yes", "no")
| where unlawful="yes" OR lawful_basis="unknown"
description: Reconciles consent / lawful-basis records with actual processing events, flagging processing performed without a valid Art. 6 basis.
value: SA PDPL Art. 6 requires a lawful basis for each processing activity; the UC produces direct evidence of alignment or gaps.
implementation: (1) Emit events from the CMP with subject_id + purpose_id + lawful_basis; (2) downstream systems must log processing events with the same purpose_id; (3) schedule daily. Indexes required: `index=app_processing`, `index=consent`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cmp:events`, `sourcetype=processing:events`.
visualization: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner. Key fields: `]`, `consented`, `lawful_basis`, `processed`, `purpose_id`, `subject_id`.
knownFalsePositives: Tier-2 framework drift during quarterly reviews, scheduled crosswalk updates, or controlled exception cycles.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Consent management platform logs, downstream processing system logs tagged with purpose..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Emit events from the CMP with subject_id + purpose_id + lawful_basis; (2) downstream systems must log processing events with the same purpose_id; (3) schedule daily.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=consent sourcetype=cmp:events earliest=-24h
| eval lawful_basis=coalesce(lawful_basis,"unknown")
| stats count AS consented BY subject_id, purpose_id, lawful_basis
| join type=left purpose_id
    [ search index=app_processing earliest=-24h sourcetype=processing:events | stats count AS processed BY subject_id, purpose_id ]
| eval unlawful=if(isnotnull(processed) AND consented=0, "yes", "no")
| where unlawful="yes" OR lawful_basis="unknown"
```

Understanding this SPL

**SA PDPL Art. 6 — processing-purpose and lawful-basis evidence** — SA PDPL Art. 6 requires a lawful basis for each processing activity; the UC produces direct evidence of alignment or gaps.

Documented **Data sources**: Consent management platform logs, downstream processing system logs tagged with purpose. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: consent; **sourcetype**: cmp:events. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=consent, sourcetype=cmp:events, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **lawful_basis** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `stats` aggregates events by **subject_id, purpose_id, lawful_basis**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **unlawful** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where unlawful="yes" OR lawful_basis="unknown"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: ], consented, lawful_basis, processed, purpose_id, subject_id, unlawful.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with the crosswalk for the tier-2 framework clause, so monitoring evidence matches the mapped control.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of flagged records for investigation; single-value KPI of coverage / compliance percentage; time chart of trend over the last 90 days; drill-down per responsible owner.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show how a second-line framework line maps to what you actually monitor, so your gap story stays clear.
